description: Search your vault locally with QMD semantic and keyword search. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

name: QMD Semantic Search

releases.author: winboost
releases.description:
Adjust remaining UI text to sentence case.
Also updates the production/qmd-semantic-search bundle to 0.1.8.

releases.author: winboost
releases.description:
Show progress for qmd actions.
Added progress modals to create collection, update index only, generate embeddings, force rebuild embeddings, and refresh semantic index.
Kept prepare vault on its existing guided progress modal.
Updated the production/qmd-semantic-search bundle to 0.1.7.

releases.author: winboost
releases.description: Add visible progress for refresh semantic index.\n\nRunning the refresh command now opens a progress modal showing collection setup, markdown indexing, embedding generation, and final status so users are not left wondering whether qmd is still working.

releases.author: winboost
releases.description: Serialize qmd CLI invocations across Obsidian windows/vaults.\n\nThis adds a small cross-process lock around qmd commands so two plugin instances cannot start qmd at the same time. It should prevent database locked/sqlite-vec errors caused by multiple vaults or Obsidian windows invoking qmd concurrently.

releases.author: winboost
releases.description:
Improve handling of temporary SQLite locks.

- Run qmd setup status commands sequentially instead of concurrently.
- Retry qmd status/list commands when SQLite reports SQLITE_BUSY/database locked.

This should avoid transient sqlite-vec/database locked errors during setup/status refreshes.

releases.author: winboost
releases.description:
Fix SQLite lock contention during embedding generation.
The plugin no longer polls qmd status while qmd embed is running, avoiding concurrent SQLite access that could surface sqlite-vec/database locked errors.

releases.author: winboost
releases.description:
Address Obsidian review feedback.

- Fixed process error handling template literal.
- Avoided returning promises from void callbacks.
- Replaced direct progress bar style updates with CSS custom properties via setCssProps.
- Updated UI copy to sentence case and removed plugin name from settings heading.
- Made openSearch synchronous.

releases.author: winboost
releases.description:
# QMD Semantic Search 0.1.1

Release prepared for Obsidian community plugin submission.

## Install manually

Download `qmd-semantic-search.zip`, extract it into:

```text
<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/
```

Final folder structure:

```text
<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/qmd-semantic-search/main.js
<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/qmd-semantic-search/manifest.json
<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/qmd-semantic-search/styles.css
```

Then enable **QMD Semantic Search** in Obsidian.

## Requirements

Install QMD locally first:

```bash
bun install -g @tobilu/qmd
```

or:

```bash
npm install -g @tobilu/qmd
```

## Highlights

- Local QMD-powered semantic and keyword search.
- No remote API calls, telemetry, or cloud LLM usage in the plugin.
- Vault-local QMD database/config.
- Manual refresh only; no background indexing while writing.
- Guided first-run setup with progress and cancel/resume.
- Search opens instantly; fast keyword results appear first, semantic refinement follows briefly.
- Click results to open the note and jump near the relevant in-note section.

See the README for the full guide.
